IELTS Academic Writing

Lesson 2 — Overview Writing Mastery

Level: B2
Duration: 90 minutes
Task: Writing Task 1
Target: Band 5.0–6.0
By the end of this lesson, you will be able to:
  • Identify graph type (trend vs. comparison)
  • Select the 2 most important key features
  • Write a clear, band-appropriate overview (1–2 sentences, no numbers)
01
Warm-up: What Is an Overview?
⏱ 10 minutes · Recognition

Read each pair of sentences. Select the correct overview sentence (no numbers, no minor details).

Question 1

Question 2

Question 3

Complete the Rule

An overview describes

It does NOT include

RECOGNITION → ANALYSIS
02
Graph Type Recognition
⏱ 10 minutes · Analysis

Classify each graph as Trend (change over time) or Comparison (categories compared, no time axis). Give a brief reason.

Graph Description Type Reason
Graph 1 Tourist attractions visited (1980–2005)
Graph 2 Overweight adults: males vs females (1990–2010)
Graph 3 Consumer goods spending: UK vs France (bar chart)
Graph 4 GDP by sector: 2016, 2017 and 2018 (table)
Graph 5 Cultural participation by age group (table)
CRITICAL THINKING TRAINING
03
Feature Selection Training
⏱ 15 minutes · Critical Thinking

Select the 2 most important features for each graph. Remember: choose overall trends and dominant comparisons — NOT specific numbers or minor details.

Graph 1 — Tourist Attractions in Scotland (line graph, 1980–2010)

Graph 2 — Overweight Australian Men and Women (bar chart, 1980–2010)

Graph 3 — Expenditure on Consumer Goods: France vs UK, 2010

Graph 4 — GDP by Economic Sector: 2016, 2017 and 2018

Graph 5 — Participation in Cultural Activities, by Age Group

CONTROLLED PRODUCTION
04
Controlled Writing — Sentence Completion
⏱ 15 minutes · Controlled Production

Complete each overview sentence using the prompts. Use appropriate academic vocabulary. No numbers.

Graph 1 — Tourist Attractions in Scotland

Overall, increased significantly before , while showed a general decline.

Graph 2 — Overweight Australian Men and Women

Overall, both groups , with remaining higher throughout the period.

Graph 3 — Consumer Goods: France vs UK

Overall, spent more than in most categories, especially on .

Graph 4 — GDP by Sector: 2016–2018

Overall, was the dominant sector throughout, whereas remained the smallest contributor.

Graph 5 — Cultural Participation by Age Group

Overall, participated more in most activities, while showed notably higher interest in .
FREE PRODUCTION
05
Independent Overview Writing
⏱ 20 minutes · Free Production

Write a 1–2 sentence overview for each graph entirely on your own. Focus on the 2 key features you identified. No numbers. No specific details.

LANGUAGE UPGRADE
06
Upgrade to Band 7 — Vocabulary Focus
⏱ 10 minutes · Lexical Resource

Replace each basic word or phrase with a more academic, precise alternative.

increase →
go down →
very big →
stay the same →
highest →
lowest →

Now improve this weak sentence:

ORIGINAL — Band 5

"Overall, the number went up and then went down."

PIE CHART EXTENSION
08
Pie Chart Snapshot — Feature Selection
⏱ 10 minutes · Recognition

Pie charts need the same skill: find the 2 most dominant features. Select them below.

Pie Chart 1 — Books Sold by a Bookseller (1972, 1992, 2012)

Pie Chart 2 — What Makes People Happy, by Age Group

09
Pie Chart — Comparison Linking
⏱ 10 minutes · Controlled Production

Use comparison language to write one overview sentence per chart. Use the phrase box below or your own words.

USEFUL PHRASES

… accounted for the largest share  ·  … was the most prominent category  ·  … represented a considerably smaller proportion  ·  while … remained negligible  ·  … saw a notable shift between the two periods  ·  the ranking changed significantly

Pie Chart 1 — Books Sold (1972–2012)

Pie Chart 2 — Happiness Factors by Age Group

10
Write Full Pie Chart Overviews
⏱ 10 minutes · Free Production

Write a complete 1–2 sentence overview for each pie chart independently. No notes, no prompts.

CRITICAL ANALYSIS
11
Strong vs. Weak Features
⏱ 8 minutes · Evaluation

Mark each feature as Strong (worth including in an overview) or Weak (too detailed or minor). Then explain why weak features hurt your score.

FeatureStrongWeak
The largest category clearly dominates
The exact percentage is 34%
There is a clear overall trend across the period
One category changed by 2%
The ranking of categories reversed between periods
A minor subcategory is 3% smaller than another
12
Upgrade to Band 6 — Sentence Improvement
⏱ 8 minutes · Lexical Resource

These sentences would score Band 4–5. Rewrite each one to reach Band 6 — use precise vocabulary and avoid vague language.

Sentence 1

WEAK — BAND 4

Overall, A is bigger than B.

Sentence 2

WEAK — BAND 4

Overall, the number changes over time.

Sentence 3

WEAK — BAND 4

Overall, there are many differences.

13
Self & Peer Assessment
⏱ 10 minutes · Reflection

Swap your pie chart overviews (Ex 10) with a partner. Assess each other, then discuss one improvement.

TR — Task Response

CC — Coherence & Cohesion

LR — Lexical Resource

GR — Grammatical Range

PEER FEEDBACK — ONE THING TO IMPROVE
REFLECTION
07
Self-Assessment Checklist
⏱ 10 minutes · Metacognition

Review all your written overviews from this lesson. Tick honestly what you achieved overall.

TR — Task Response

CC — Coherence & Cohesion

LR — Lexical Resource

GR — Grammatical Range

MY TARGET FOR NEXT LESSON
FINAL INSIGHT

In this lesson, you are not simply practising writing.

You are learning to filter data, identify patterns, and summarise logically.

This is the foundation of Band 5.0–6.0 — and the path beyond.

Timing Guide: Ex 1–3 (concept + recognition) → Ex 4–7 (line/bar: controlled → free → upgrade → reflection) → Ex 8–10 (pie chart extension) → Ex 11–12 (evaluation + upgrade) → Ex 13 (peer assessment).

Key Intervention Points:

  • Students select details instead of trends → correct immediately in Ex 3 and Ex 8
  • Students include specific numbers → stop them before Ex 6 and Ex 10
  • Students confuse single vs comparative pie charts → clarify in Ex 8
  • Vague overviews ("there are differences") → redirect to Ex 12 before Ex 10
  • Weak vocabulary → redirect to Ex 9 phrase box before Ex 10

Peer Assessment (Ex 13): Give 3 minutes each way. Encourage specific written feedback, not just ticks.
